sections.paragraph:
ἄδικος, ον (Hes.+) ‘unjust’.

① pert. to acting in a way that is contrary to what is right, unjust, crooked of pers.: opp. δίκαιος model citizen (Aristot., Cat. 10b, 15 al.; Epict. 2, 11, 5; Aesop, Fab. 173 P.=308 H.; Philo, Abr. 33; Jos., Bell. 2, 139; 5, 407) Mt 5:45; Ac 24:15; 1 Pt 3:18; Dg 9:2; Hm 6, 1, 1f. W. ἄνομος (X., Mem. 4, 4, 13) 1 Cl 56:11 (Job 5:22). W. ἅρπαγες and μοιχοί Lk 18:11. Negative of God Ro 3:5; Hb 6:10. The ἄ. is excluded fr. the Kingdom 1 Cor 6:9; ἄ. become rich 2 Cl 20:1. In a statement critical not of secular judges but, as the opp. term ἅγιοι indicates, of the litigious conduct of Paul’s addressees κρίνεσθαι ἐπὶ τῶν ἄ. go to law before the unjust (that is, ‘those who are from your Christian perspective impious unbelievers’) 1 Cor 6:1 (Maximus Tyr. 3, 4c ὡς ἐπὶ δικαστῶν; ἀλλὰ ἄδικοι). In MPol 19:2 a proconsul’s judgment is declared to be unjust. Opp. εὐσεβής 2 Pt 2:9 (Diod S 4, 2, 6 ἀδίκους κ. ἀσεβεῖς; Jos., Ant. 8, 251 ἄδ. κ. ἀσεβής); dishonest, untrustworthy (opp. πιστός) ὁ ἐν ἐλαχίστῳ ἄ. καὶ ἐν πολλῷ ἄ. ἐστιν Lk 16:10. Of Satan AcPlCor 2:11 (s. ἄρχων 1c).

② pert. to being associated w. injustice, unjust of things (Phalaris, Ep. 70 κέρδος ἄ.; Pr 15:26; Jos., Vi. 299) μαμωνᾶς Lk 16:9 D, 11 (s. ἀδικία 2.—πλοῦτος ἄ. of ill-gotten gains: Chariton 3, 3, 11; Philo, Spec. Leg. 4, 158 and the opp. πλοῦτος δίκαιος SibOr 3, 783); κρίσις ἄ. (TestJos 14:1) Pol 6:1; ἄ. βίος (w. ἄνομος) MPol 3:1; ζῆλος ἄ. 1 Cl 3:4; 5:4; 45:4; ἄ. συγγραφή B 3:3 (Is 58:6). οὐδὲν ἄ. 1 Cl 45:3.—Lit. s.v. ἀδικία.—DELG s.v. δίκη. M-M. TW.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

pers. pers. = person(s)

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. fr. = from

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Maximus Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 21
